steps.title: Identify the boundary line from the graph
steps.content: The dashed line passes through the y-intercept $(0, -1)$ and another point on the line is $(1, -4)$. The slope is $\frac{-4 - (-1)}{1 - 0} = \frac{-3}{1} = -3$. The boundary line equation is $y = -1 - 3x$.

steps.title: Test a point to determine the inequality direction
steps.content:
The shaded region includes the point $(0, 0)$. Test whether it satisfies $y > -1 - 3x$:
$$0 > -1 - 3(0)$$$$0 > -1$$ ✓
This is true, so the shaded region is where $y$ values are greater than the boundary line. The dashed line (not solid) confirms the inequality is strict. That matches choice D — D is correct.
